findings.evidence: normalize_apply_zero (line 70) is @[simp] with the same product as the inner subterm of the _re/_im lemmas; Complex.one_re/one_im are simp in Mathlib.
findings.fix: Drop the @[simp] on normalize_apply_zero_re/_im; given they are trivial corollaries of normalize_apply_zero they can likely be removed entirely rather than kept as plain lemmas.
findings.issue: normalize_apply_zero_re and normalize_apply_zero_im are marked @[simp], but since normalize_apply_zero (also @[simp]) rewrites the inner product `(((F 0).re)⁻¹ : ℂ) * F 0` to `1` first (simp normalizes subterms bottom-up), `(…).re` and `(…).im` reduce via the existing `Complex.one_re`/`Complex.one_im`. These two simp lemmas can therefore never fire usefully — when hF/h0 are in scope the inner term is already rewritten, and when they are not, none of the three can fire.

findings.evidence: Its only use site is `hF.const_mul hF.normalizeScalar_nonneg` in `normalize` (line 68); the fact `0 ≤ ((F 0).re)⁻¹` is otherwise trivially derivable.
findings.fix: Mark it `private` (the public surface is `normalize` plus the value/bound lemmas).
findings.issue: normalizeScalar_nonneg is an implementation helper consumed only by `normalize` (one-line `inv_nonneg.mpr …`); exposing it adds surface no named downstream target needs.

summary: The characteristic API is otherwise complete and bodies are correctly hidden, but two of the three @[simp] normalization lemmas are redundant simp annotations and one helper is over-exposed.
